sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the current state:
- The white containers on the right side (the 40-pixel white objects) have disappeared
- The red T-shape (47px) and blue L-shape (47px) are still there on the right
- The bottom timer bar is now 39px (was 40px)
- The white divider blocks are still there
- Left side objects are unchanged
So clicking the red T-shape removed the white containers on the right side AND shrunk the timer by 1. The timer went from 40 to 39.
Now I need to figure out what to do next. The right side still has the blue L-shape. Let me try clicking it to see if it has a similar effect.
But wait - I should also check if the red T-shape can be clicked again, or if clicking the blue L-shape does something different.
Looking at the image, the right side now has the red T-shape and blue L-shape without white outlines. The timer is at 39.
Let me try clicking the blue L-shape to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# White containers on right disappeared after clicking red T-shape
# Timer went from 40 to 39
# Blue L-shape still exists at [[34, 45], 47px]
# Let me click the blue L-shape to see what happens

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 48}])
print(f"Blue L-shape click: board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed', False)}, done={last_action_result.get('done', False)}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

# Check timer after
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 20:
        print(f"Timer bar: {n['pixels']}px")
</parameter>

Left-side squares shrink timer by 1-2.
